decisions.reasoning: First session, no prior journal context. MSFT down 12.27% from entry ($425.31 to $373.11). Technical downtrend confirmed: fast SMA $384 well below slow SMA $409. New securities class action lawsuit filed June 23 (deadline Aug 11, alleging fraud re Azure performance and Copilot adoption) adds headline risk. However, analyst consensus target ~$562-570 (Strong Buy, 32 analysts) and Azure AI thesis remain intact long-term. No new invalidation of fundamental thesis; trimming at the low without a catalyst would lock in loss. Holding small position (12% of equity).

sources.headline: MSFT class action lawsuit deadline August 11 — securities fraud allegations re Azure performance and Copilot adoption
MSFT consensus analyst target ~$562-570, Strong Buy from 32 analysts
BofA forecasts 3 Fed rate hikes in 2026 (Sept/Oct/Dec) — headwind for high-multiple tech

decisions.invalidation: Close below $360 further -4%, or Azure revenue growth decelerates below 30% next quarter, or class action achieves class certification with substantive fraud findings

decisions.reasoning: NVDA down 8.00% from entry ($217.09 to $199.72). Q1 FY2027 results (May 20) were a blowout: revenue $81.6B beat, EPS $1.87 beat, Blackwell GPUs sold out, data center +69% YoY. Analyst consensus target $298-310 implies 50%+ upside. Holding through two near-term binary events: Micron earnings tonight (June 24 AH, direct read-through for AI memory demand) and PCE data Thursday (June 26, expected hot at 4.1% headline). A hot PCE accelerates rate-hike bets and compresses NVDA multiple. Will reassess after both events clear. Largest position at 32% of equity.

sources.headline: Nvidia Q1 FY2027: Revenue $81.6B beat, EPS $1.87 beat, Blackwell sold out, data center +69% YoY
Micron reports Q3 earnings tonight June 24 AH — binary event for AI memory and semiconductor sentiment
May PCE expected +0.5% MoM, 4.1% headline annual — hot print would fuel rate-hike bets and hurt tech multiples

decisions.invalidation: Micron miss with weak HBM guidance tonight, or PCE prints above 4.5% accelerating hike timeline, or export controls expand to Blackwell H20

decisions.reasoning: QQQ down 3.79% from entry ($742.88 to $714.71). SMA signals show fast SMA (723.38) just crossed above slow SMA (722.42) — a fresh long crossover, technically constructive but thin. 36 of 46 QQQ companies beat Q1 2026 estimates. Near-term binary events (MU earnings tonight, PCE Thursday) dominate technical picture. Holding as diversified tech exposure rather than adding single-name concentration.

sources.headline: QQQ fast SMA (723.38) just crossed above slow SMA (722.42) — long signal
36 of 46 QQQ companies beat Q1 2026 earnings, AI-driven tech showing fundamental strength

decisions.invalidation: MU miss causing Nasdaq gap-down >2% overnight, or PCE print causes fast SMA to cross back below slow SMA

decisions.reasoning: SPY essentially flat from entry (+0.35%). Broad market anchor. S&P 500 bouncing +0.03% today after -1.44% selloff yesterday triggered by BofA rate hike forecast and KOSPI double-digit decline. Holding as defensive broad-market exposure; SPY less rate-sensitive than QQQ/NVDA/MSFT given diversified sector exposure.

sources.headline: S&P 500 up ~0.35% today, modest recovery from semiconductor-led selloff
S&P 500 fell 1.44% June 23, triggered by BofA rate hike forecast and South Korea KOSPI double-digit decline

decisions.invalidation: PCE print triggers >2% S&P decline or MU earnings miss cascades into broader market selloff

decisions.reasoning: Preserving $853 cash (9.1% of equity) ahead of two back-to-back binary events: Micron earnings tonight and PCE data Thursday June 26. Deploying into risk positions immediately before these catalysts would be FOMO-driven. Will reassess deployment after events clear — may add to NVDA or QQQ on constructive outcome.

sources.headline: May PCE expected hot Thursday June 26 — would accelerate rate-hike bets
Micron Q3 earnings tonight June 24 — proxy for AI memory and data center demand

decisions.invalidation: MU beats strongly tonight AND tech rallies >2%, creating momentum opportunity to deploy cash into existing positions

summary: First session: inherited 4 positions (MSFT -12.27%, NVDA -8.00%, QQQ -3.79%, SPY +0.35%). All held — two major binary catalysts ahead (MU earnings tonight, PCE Thursday June 26). Preserving $853 cash pending catalysts. Macro: Fed dot plot shifted to hike, BofA forecasts 3 hikes, Iran energy crisis driving stagflation, tariffs.
open_theses: NVDA: AI demand structural, Blackwell sold out, Q1 FY2027 blowout — hold for consensus target $298-310; consider adding on dip if MU beats and PCE is benign
MSFT: Long-term Azure AI thesis intact despite -12% drawdown; monitor class action Aug 11 deadline and Copilot adoption; would add on confirming Azure acceleration
QQQ: Fresh SMA long crossover signal; diversified tech exposure; watching PCE Thursday as key directional trigger
SPY: Defensive anchor; would consider trimming if PCE causes Fed expectations to reprice to >3 hikes in accelerated timeline
